key_insight: DrugGen-2 is a disease-aware generative model that combines disease ontology and protein sequence context to design new small molecules more effectively than prior baselines.
clinical_relevance: By incorporating disease context, this approach may better support discovery and repurposing of candidate drugs for complex disease settings, potentially improving lead prioritization.
method: Fine-tuned a GPT-2 model on approved drug–disease–target data.
Used supervised fine-tuning followed by GRPO reinforcement learning.
Optimized rewards for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and predicted binding affinity.
evidence_signal: In evaluations on five diabetic nephropathy–relevant protein targets, DrugGen-2 outperformed DrugGPT and DrugGen, with docking candidates showing predicted affinities as strong as -9.917 versus -8.283 for a reference drug.
watch_item: Results rely on predicted affinity and docking rather than experimental validation, so real-world effectiveness and safety remain unconfirmed.
paper_title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
